children.heading: Wireless emergency alerts system offerings
children.label: a Wireless emergency alerts system offerings

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: 2 Regulations
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the Commission, in consultation with the Administrator, shall adopt regulations to implement the amendment made by paragraph (1)(B).January 1, 20211See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: State emergency alert system plans and emergency communications committees
children.label: b State emergency alert system plans and emergency communications committees
i
children.text: to establish an SECC if the State does not have an SECC; or

children.label: ii
children.text: if the State has an SECC, to review the composition and governance of the SECC;

children.label: I
children.text: meet to review and update its State EAS Plan;

children.label: II
children.text: certify to the Commission that the SECC has met as required under subclause (I); and

children.label: III
children.text: submit to the Commission an updated State EAS Plan; and

children.label: I
children.text: approve or disapprove the updated State EAS Plan; and

children.label: II
children.text: notify the chief executive of the State of the Commission’s approval or disapproval of such plan, and reason therefor; and

children.label: C
children.text: establish a State EAS Plan content checklist for SECCs to use when reviewing and updating a State EAS Plan for submission to the Commission under subparagraph (B)(i).

children.heading: Consultation
children.label: 2 Consultation
children.text: The Commission shall consult with the Administrator regarding the adoption of regulations under paragraph (1)(C).

children.label: A
children.text: the term “SECC” means a State Emergency Communications Committee;

children.label: B
children.text: the term “State” means any State of the United States, the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, the United States Virgin Islands, Guam, American Samoa, the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands, and any possession of the United States; and

children.label: C
children.text: the term “State EAS Plan” means a State Emergency Alert System Plan.

children.heading: False alert reporting
children.label: c False alert reporting
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the Commission, in consultation with the Administrator, shall complete a rulemaking proceeding to establish a system to receive from the Administrator or State, Tribal, or local governments reports of false alerts under the Emergency Alert System or the Wireless Emergency Alerts System for the purpose of recording such false alerts and examining the causes of such false alerts.January 1, 2021

children.heading: Repeating emergency alert system messages for national security
children.label: d Repeating emergency alert system messages for national security
A
children.text: the President;

children.label: B
children.text: the Administrator; or

children.label: C
children.text: any other entity determined appropriate under the circumstances by the Commission, in consultation with the Administrator.

children.label: A
children.text: apply to warnings of national security events, meaning emergencies of national significance, such as a missile threat, terror attack, or other act of war or threat to public safety; and

children.label: B
children.text: not apply to more typical warnings, such as a weather alert, AMBER Alert, or disaster alert.

children.label: A
children.text: the authority of the President granted by law to alert and warn the public; or

children.label: B
children.text: the role of the President as commander-in-chief with respect to the identification, dissemination, notification, or alerting of information of missile threats against the United States, or threats to public safety.

children.heading: Internet and online streaming services emergency alert examination
children.label: e Internet and online streaming services emergency alert examination

children.heading: Study
children.label: 1 Study
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , and after providing public notice and opportunity for comment, the Commission shall complete an inquiry to examine the feasibility of updating the Emergency Alert System to enable or improve alerts to consumers provided through the internet, including through streaming services.January 1, 2021

children.label: A
children.text: the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate; and

children.label: B
children.text: the Committee on Energy and Commerce of the House of Representatives.
the term “Administrator” means the Administrator of the Federal Emergency Management Agency;
the term “Commission” means the Federal Communications Commission;
the term “Emergency Alert System” means the national public warning system, the rules for which are set forth in part 11 of title 47, Code of Federal Regulations (or any successor regulation); and

the term “Wireless Emergency Alerts System” means the wireless national public warning system established under the Warning, Alert, and Response Network Act ( et seq.), the rules for which are set forth in part 10 of title 47, Code of Federal Regulations (or any successor regulation).47 U.S.C. 1201
